title: quercus virginiana subsp. dentata
intro: Quercus virginiana subsp. dentata, also known as the southern live oak dwarf or dwarf live oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Quercus virginiana subsp. dentata include its tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Quercus virginiana subsp. dentata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
